stimulus:
The following text is adapted from Edith Wharton's 1920 novel The Age of Innocence. Mr. Archer is planning a meeting with a friend.

He had already resolved to seek her out at the opera, where he was sure to find her in her usual box. He had only to fix the moment during the evening when he would approach. He decided to wait through the first act and slip away during the second intermission.
stem: As used in the text, what does the word “fix” most nearly mean?
rationale: Choice B is the best answer because "settle" most nearly captures the sense of deciding or pinning down, which matches the narrator's process of choosing exactly when to approach. Choice A is incorrect because "repair" means to mend something broken; nothing in the text needs repair. Choice C is incorrect because "restrict" means to limit; the narrator is selecting a time, not constraining one. Choice D is incorrect because "fasten" means to attach physically; the moment is a point in time, not an object to be attached.
